description_long: This object is a large, multi-barreled rotary rifle mounted on a sturdy, three-legged tripod base. The main body is cylindrical with metallic and brass components, featuring a visible ammunition belt feeding into the chamber from a side-mounted box. The barrels are arranged in a Gatling-style configuration, suggesting a high rate of fire, and are supported by a robust frame with mechanical details and exposed gears. Decorative elements, such as a shield with a patriotic motif, are present on the side, adding a historical or ceremonial touch. The tripod legs are curved and reinforced, providing stability for the heavy weapon. The overall construction is predominantly metal, with a mix of dark, bronze, and steel tones, and the design is reminiscent of late 19th to early 20th-century heavy machine guns.
description: A large, tripod-mounted, multi-barreled rotary rifle with a visible ammo belt and decorative shield, constructed mainly from dark metals and brass.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular arrangement of the barrels and the central axis of the rotary mechanism.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the barrels, the ammo belt, and the decorative shield on the body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the main body, the ammo box, and the tripod's rear leg.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the shield's design and the full length of the barrels from another angle.
